types.name: AuditLogCursor
types.description:
A cursor for enumerating a list of audit log entries

If there are additional results available, the cursor object may be passed
back into the same endpoint to retrieve another page. If the cursor is null,
there are no remaining results to return.

types.name: BillingAddress
types.description: Billing address for invoicing.

fields.name: country
fields.description: ISO 3166 two-letter country code

fields.name: vat
fields.description: Value-added tax number (EU)

types.name: BillingAddressInput
types.description:
All fields are optional. If a field is omitted, it is unchanged. If a field is
null, it is set to null.

fields.name: autorenew
fields.description: If true, payment is automatically renewed when term ellapses.

fields.name: product
fields.description: Selected product associated with this subscription.

fields.name: price
fields.description:
Shortcut to get the applicable price point for this subscription's product ID
and applicable currency

fields.name: subtotal
fields.description:
Total price, not including applicable taxes, in the smallest denomination of
the currency, e.g. cents USD.

fields.name: total
fields.description:
Total price, including applicable taxes, in the smallest denomination of the
currency, e.g. cents USD.

fields.name: payment
fields.description: Status of the last attempted payment for this subscription.

types.name: Boolean
types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.

types.name: Country
types.description: ISO-3166 country code

fields.name: canonicalName
fields.description:
The canonical name of this entity. For users, this is their username
prefixed with '~'. Additional entity types will be supported in the future.

types.name: Features
types.description: Describes available optional features

types.name: Float
types.description: The `Float` scalar type represents signed double-precision fractional values as specified by [IEEE 754](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IEEE_floating_point).

types.name: ID
types.description: The `ID` scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable. When expected as an input type, any string (such as "4") or integer (such as 4) input value will be accepted as an ID.

types.name: Int
types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

fields.name: serviceStart
fields.description: Start of service period for which this invoice applies

fields.name: serviceEnd
fields.description: End of service period for which this invoice applies

fields.name: total
fields.description:
Amount charged denoted in the smallest denomination of the applicable
currency, e.g. cents USD.

types.name: InvoiceCursor
types.description:
A cursor for enumerating a list of invoices

If there are additional results available, the cursor object may be passed
back into the same endpoint to retrieve another page. If the cursor is null,
there are no remaining results to return.

fields.name: totp
fields.description: True if TOTP (time-based one time passwords) is enabled for 2FA

fields.name: createSSHKey
fields.description:
Creates a new SSH public key from an input as used in the authorized_keys
file, see the AUTHORIZED_KEYS FILE FORMAT section in sshd(8). Note that
the optional key options are currently discarded.

fields.name: updateSSHKeyLastUsed
fields.description: Causes the "last used" time of this SSH key to be set to the current time.

fields.name: updateBillingAddress
fields.description: Update the authenticated user's billing address.

fields.name: updateBillingSubscription
fields.description: Update an active billing subscription.

fields.name: cancelBillingSubscription
fields.description:
Cancels an active billing subscription. The changes will not take effect
until the end of the paid term (unless the account is past due, in which case
it applies immediately).

fields.name: deletePaymentMethod
fields.description:
Removes a payment method from your account. Cannot be used to remove the last
payment method.

fields.name: setDefaultPaymentMethod
fields.description: Sets the user's default payment method.

fields.name: createWebhook
fields.description:
Creates a new user profile webhook subscription. When an event from the
provided list of events occurs, the 'query' parameter (a GraphQL query)
will be evaluated and the results will be sent to the provided URL as the
body of an HTTP POST request. The list of events must include at least one
event, and no duplicates.

This query is evaluated in the webhook context, such that query { webhook }
may be used to access details of the event which trigged the webhook. The
query may not make any mutations.

fields.name: deleteWebhook
fields.description:
Deletes a user profile webhook. Any events already queued may still be
delivered after this request completes. Clients authenticated with a
personal access token may delete any webhook registered for their account,
but authorized OAuth 2.0 clients may only delete their own webhooks.
Manually deleting a webhook configured by a third-party client may cause
unexpected behavior with the third-party integration.

fields.name: billingTransfer
fields.description:
Transfers billing details from one user account to another, returning the
target account.

fields.name: subsidizeUser
fields.description: Grants free service to the given user ID until the provided expiration date.

fields.name: createProduct
fields.description: Create a new paid product.

fields.name: retireProduct
fields.description: Retires a paid product.

fields.name: registerAccount
fields.description: Registers a new account.

fields.name: resetPassword
fields.description: Requests a password reset. The return value is meaningless.

fields.name: confirmRegistration
fields.description: Confirm an account registration email.

fields.name: confirmEmailChange
fields.description: Confirm a user's change of email address

fields.name: confirmPasswordChange
fields.description: Confirm a user's password change

fields.name: enableTOTP
fields.description: Enables TOTP for the user account. The secret is 20 base32-encoded bytes.

fields.name: registerOAuthClient
fields.description:
Registers an OAuth client. Only OAuth 2.0 confidental clients are
supported.

fields.name: revokeOAuthClient
fields.description:
Revokes this OAuth client, revoking all tokens for it and preventing future
use.

fields.name: revokeOAuthGrant
fields.description: Revokes a specific OAuth grant.

fields.name: issuePersonalAccessToken
fields.description: Issues an OAuth personal access token.

fields.name: revokePersonalAccessToken
fields.description: Revokes a personal access token.

fields.name: issueAuthorizationCode
fields.description:
Issues an OAuth 2.0 authorization code. Used after the user has consented
to the access grant request.

fields.name: issueOAuthGrant
fields.description:
Completes the OAuth 2.0 grant process and issues an OAuth token for a
specific OAuth client.

fields.name: refreshOAuthGrant
fields.description:
Refreshes an existing OAuth 2.0 grant. This invalidates the previous grant
and returns a new one.

fields.name: sendEmail
fields.description:
Send a notification email.

The 'address' parameter must be a single RFC 5322 address (e.g. "Barry Gibbs
<bg@example.com>", or "bg@example.com"). The 'message' parameter must be a
RFC 5322 compliant Internet message with the special requirement that it must
not contain any recipients (i.e. no 'To', 'Cc', or 'Bcc' header).

The message will be signed with the site key. If the address is that of a
registered user it will be encrypted according to the user's privacy
settings.

fields.name: deleteUser
fields.description: Deletes the authenticated user's account.

fields.name: orderProductSubscription
fields.description:
Orders a new paid product subscription. The new subscription is in a PENDING
state. The payment intent should be completed to proceed.

fields.name: beginSubscriptionRenewal
fields.description:
Creates a new payment intent to charge a subscription's renewal fee
interactively. The subscription must be past due.

fields.name: finalizePaymentIntent
fields.description:
Finalizes a product order from the PaymentIntent ID after the user has
finalized their payment, returning the completed PaymentIntent.

fields.name: createSetupIntent
fields.description:
Creates a "setup" intent for adding a new payment method to the authenticated
user's account.

fields.name: finalizeSetupIntent
fields.description: Finalizes a setup intent.

fields.name: processPaymentDue
fields.description:
Processes payment when the user's account is past due. Returns the payment
intent if a payment was collected, or null if a payment was not collected but
the operation was successful (the latter occurs when a subscription was
cancelled or a subsidized user's subsidy expired, in these cases the account
is settled and converted to non-paying).

fields.name: reissueInvoice
fields.description:
Causes an invoice to be regenerated in order to issue a correction. The admin
is assumed to have manually made the required changes in the database.

types.name: PGPKeyCursor
types.description:
A cursor for enumerating a list of PGP keys

If there are additional results available, the cursor object may be passed
back into the same endpoint to retrieve another page. If the cursor is null,
there are no remaining results to return.

fields.name: currency
fields.description: Currency used with this payment method

fields.name: name
fields.description: User-friendly name of this payment method (e.g. 'Visa ending in 1234')

fields.name: expires
fields.description: Expiration date of this payment method, if applicable to the type

types.name: Product
types.description: A paid product available for purchase.

fields.name: retired
fields.description: This product has been retired and is no longer available for purchase.

fields.name: subsidized
fields.description:
This price is subsidized and available to users who cannot pay the standard
prices.

types.name: ProductPrice
types.description: Price point for a product in a given currency.

fields.name: currency
fields.description: Applicable currency for this price

fields.name: amount
fields.description:
Price in the smallest denomination of the currency, e.g. cents USD. Does not
include any applicable taxes.

fields.name: version
fields.description: Returns API version information.

fields.name: me
fields.description: Returns the authenticated user.

fields.name: userByName
fields.description: Returns a specific user

fields.name: sshKey
fields.description: Returns a specific SSH key by its resource ID.

fields.name: pgpKey
fields.description: Returns a specific PGP key by its resource ID.

fields.name: sshKeyByFingerprint
fields.description: Returns a specific SSH key by its fingerprint, in hexadecimal

fields.name: pgpKeyByFingerprint
fields.description: Returns a specific PGP key by its fingerprint, in hexadecimal.

fields.name: auditLog
fields.description: Returns the audit log for the authenticated user.

fields.name: loginSecurity
fields.description: Returns the authenticated user's login security settings.

fields.name: profileWebhooks
fields.description:
Returns a list of user profile webhook subscriptions. For clients
authenticated with a personal access token, this returns all webhooks
configured by all GraphQL clients for your account. For clients
authenticated with an OAuth 2.0 access token, this returns only webhooks
registered for your client.

fields.name: profileWebhook
fields.description: Returns details of a user profile webhook subscription by its ID.

fields.name: webhook
fields.description:
Returns information about the webhook currently being processed. This is
not valid during normal queries over HTTP, and will return an error if used
outside of a webhook context.

fields.name: myOauthGrant
fields.description: Returns the current OAuth grant in use, if any

fields.name: oauthGrants
fields.description: Returns OAuth grants issued for the authenticated user

fields.name: oauthClients
fields.description: List of OAuth clients this user administrates

fields.name: personalAccessTokens
fields.description: Returns a list of personal OAuth tokens issued

fields.name: product
fields.description: Returns information about a subscription product available for purchase.

fields.name: products
fields.description: Returns a list of paid subscriptions available for purchase.

fields.name: userByID
fields.description: Returns a specific user by ID

fields.name: user
fields.description: Returns a specific user by username

fields.name: oauthClientByID
fields.description: Returns a specific OAuth client (by database ID)

fields.name: oauthClientByUUID
fields.description: Returns a specific OAuth client (by UUID)

fields.name: tokenRevocationStatus
fields.description:
Returns the revocation status of a given OAuth 2.0 token hash (SHA-512). If
the token or client ID has been revoked, this returns true, and the key
should not be trusted. Client ID is optional for personal access tokens.

types.name: SSHKey
types.description: An SSH public key associated with an account.

fields.name: key
fields.description: The base64-encoded public key

fields.name: keyType
fields.description: The key type

fields.name: fingerprint
fields.description: The legacy MD5 fingerprint of the key.

fields.name: fingerprintSHA256
fields.description: The SHA256 fingerprint of the key.

types.name: SSHKeyCursor
types.description:
A cursor for enumerating a list of SSH keys

If there are additional results available, the cursor object may be passed
back into the same endpoint to retrieve another page. If the cursor is null,
there are no remaining results to return.

types.name: String
types.description: The `String`scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

fields.name: recoveryCodes
fields.description: List of generated recovery codes for use with TOTP.

types.name: Time
types.description: String of the format %Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ

types.name: URL
types.description:
URL from which some secondary data may be retrieved.

Resources which are access protected require you to provide a valid
Authentication header when retrieving this URL. URLs are not guaranteed to be
consistent for an extended length of time; applications should submit a new
GraphQL request each time they wish to access the data at the provided URL.

types.name: UpdateBillingSubscriptionInput
types.description:
For changing the active paid subscription's parameters. Changes apply from the
start of the next payment term (i.e. from User.paymentDue).

types.name: Upload
types.description:
A file uploaded alongside the GraphQL request. This API is compatible with the
multipart request specification for requests that include uploads:

https://github.com/jaydenseric/graphql-multipart-request-spec

fields.name: paymentStatus
fields.description: User's current payment status. Only available if authenticated as this user.

fields.name: paymentDue
fields.description:
Date at which next payment is due. Only available if authenticated as this
user.

fields.name: subscription
fields.description:
Details about the user's current paid subscription. Only available if
authenticated as this user.

fields.name: invoices
fields.description: Returns invoices for this user. Only available if authenticated as this user.

fields.name: billingAddress
fields.description: User's billing address, if applicable, for invoicing.

fields.name: paymentMethods
fields.description: User's payment methods, if any.

fields.name: defaultPaymentMethod
fields.description: The default payment method in use for automatic subscription renewals.

fields.name: receivesPaidServices
fields.description: Returns true if this user should have access to paid services.

fields.name: suspensionNotice
fields.description: Notice to provide to a suspended account

types.name: UserInput
types.description:
Omit these fields to leave them unchanged, or set them to null to clear
their value.

fields.name: deprecationDate
fields.description:
If this API version is scheduled for deprecation, this is the date on which
it will stop working; or null if this API version is not scheduled for
deprecation.

fields.name: features
fields.description: Optional features

fields.name: responseBody
fields.description:
These details are provided only after a response is received from the
remote server. If a response is sent whose Content-Type is not text/*, or
cannot be decoded as UTF-8, the response body will be null. It will be
truncated after 64 KiB.

types.name: WebhookDeliveryCursor
types.description:
A cursor for enumerating a list of webhook deliveries

If there are additional results available, the cursor object may be passed
back into the same endpoint to retrieve another page. If the cursor is null,
there are no remaining results to return.

fields.name: client
fields.description:
If this webhook was registered by an authorized OAuth 2.0 client, this
field is non-null.

fields.name: deliveries
fields.description: All deliveries which have been sent to this webhook.

fields.name: sample
fields.description: Returns a sample payload for this subscription, for testing purposes

types.name: WebhookSubscriptionCursor
types.description:
A cursor for enumerating a list of webhook subscriptions

If there are additional results available, the cursor object may be passed
back into the same endpoint to retrieve another page. If the cursor is null,
there are no remaining results to return.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.
